title: SSRF guard bypassed by HTTP redirects in fetch_url
description:
## Severity
Critical
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/web.rs:262-300 (fetch_url)
## Violation
`validate_url(url)` runs on the user-supplied URL, then the request is made to `format!("{JINA_READER_URL}{url}")`. The `reqwest::Client` built at line 265-268 has default redirect policy (follow up to 10). An attacker that controls a public hostname that returns a 302 to `http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/` — or to a private RFC1918 address — completely defeats the SSRF guard: the dial-time check is never re-run on the redirect target.

```rust
pub async fn fetch_url(url: &str) -> Result<FetchResult, String> {
    validate_url(url)?;

    let client = Client::builder()
        .timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))
        .build()
```

The same problem applies to `fetch_with_browser` (line 598) — headless Chrome follows redirects with no per-hop guard.
## Mandated refactor
Build the reqwest client with `.redirect(redirect::Policy::custom(|attempt| { validate_url(attempt.url().as_str()).map(|_| attempt.follow()).unwrap_or_else(|_| attempt.stop()) }))`, or `redirect::Policy::none()` and re-`validate_url` + re-dispatch manually on every 3xx. Install a `reqwest::dns::Resolve` impl that re-checks resolved IPs against `is_ip_forbidden` at dial time to close the DNS-rebinding gap noted in the docstring at line 149-152. For headless Chrome, intercept navigation events or hard-block the page by configuring a proxy that re-validates.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
